nativeRange.habitat: Marshes, swamps, ditches, and pond margins of subtropical to tropical lowland wetlands; ranges from the southeastern United States (Texas, Florida, north to Iowa) through Mexico, the West Indies, and Central America south to Paraguay

summary: The Radican Marble Queen Sword is a large freshwater plant native to wetlands from the southern United States and Mexico south to Paraguay. Its cream-and-green marbled, heart-shaped leaves make it a natural focal point for medium to large planted aquariums.
careNotes: The Radican Marble Queen Sword (Echinodorus cordifolius) is a robust plant from subtropical and tropical American wetlands, sold in the aquarium trade under the 'Marble Queen' and 'Radican Marble Queen' names for its distinctive cream-and-green marbled leaves. Submerged plants in the aquarium typically reach 35–60 cm in height, though wild specimens growing above the waterline can reach 100 cm. Temperature of 22–28 °C (71–82 °F), pH 6.2–7.5, and moderate water hardness (gH 4–12) suit it well. This is a HEAVY ROOT FEEDER — a deep, nutrient-rich substrate is essential for the mature root mass, and regular root-tab supplementation along with iron-rich liquid fertilization is strongly recommended. CO2 injection is not strictly required but tends to support fuller leaf growth and brings out the best coloration in brighter setups. The plant works well as a midground specimen in large aquariums or as a single background centerpiece in medium-sized tanks (60–75 cm). Like other sword plants, the Radican Marble Queen grows naturally in both emersed form (broad, heart-shaped leaves above the waterline) and submerged form (longer, more lance-like leaves underwater). Plants purchased from nurseries are often grown emersed and may shed their initial leaves after being moved to a fully submerged aquarium — this is a normal transition, not a sign of poor health.
breedingNotes: The Radican Marble Queen Sword reproduces through crown division and through adventitious plantlets — sometimes called daughter plants — that develop around the base of the mother plant and along long flower or runner stalks. Once a plantlet has formed a small rosette and visible roots, it can be separated from the parent and planted directly into the substrate. The species flowers freely in wild and emersed settings from spring through fall and is capable of setting seed, though flowering and seed production are uncommon in fully submerged aquarium conditions.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Care page for Radican Marble Queen (Echinodorus cordifolius). Provided North American / Mexican native origin, temperature 71–82 °F (~22–28 °C), moderate–strong lighting, CO2 optional, moderate-to-fast growth under good conditions, midground/background placement, heavy root feeder needing iron-rich fertilization and nutrient-dense substrate, easy care level.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Echinodorus cordifolius
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (family Alismataceae, order Alismatales), full native range (Mexico, West Indies, Central America, South America to Paraguay, southeastern US north to Iowa), habitat (marshes, swamps, ditches, ponds), wild size to 100 cm, emersed-dominant growth form, and confirmation of 'Marble Queen' marbled-leaf cultivar that fades under low light. Notes that strong lighting and CO2 support best growth.

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.net — Echinodorus cordifolius
additional.notes: Filled water-parameter gaps left by Modern Aquarium: pH 6.2–7.1, hardness 4–12 dGH, temperature 22–28 °C. Submerged size 35–55 cm, steady growth, midground placement, easy care level, propagation via crown division and adventitious plantlets around the rosette base. Notes initial leaf-shedding on introduction is normal.
